title: rubus hirtus var. micropetalus
intro: Rubus hirtus var. micropetalus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a deciduous shrub native to the Himalayan region, characterized by its thorny stems and edible fruit. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Rubus hirtus var. micropetalus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate rheumatism, and support cardiovascular health. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and its fruit can also be eaten raw or used in jams and preserves.
